rules_fired.because: Thiol-based reducing agents (DTT, β-mercaptoethanol) are rapidly oxidised by dissolved O₂ in aqueous buffers. At 4°C in standard buffer, DTT has an effective half-life of approximately 1 week; at room temperature in air-exposed buffer, this drops to hours. For critical applications (disulfide bond reduction, enzyme protection), the stock should be made fresh on the day of use.
rules_fired.cite: Cleland WW (1964) Biochemistry 3:480–482 (original DTT paper, redox characterisation). Sigma-Aldrich DTT Product Information Sheet D9779.
